user.title: Set up HA Auto Dashboard
user.description: HA Auto Dashboard will scan your areas, devices and entities and keep an internal dashboard graph up to date automatically. No configuration is required.

abort.already_configured: HA Auto Dashboard is already configured.

init.title: HA Auto Dashboard options
init.description: All settings are optional - HA Auto Dashboard works with none of these set.

data_description.excluded_areas: Devices and entities in these areas are left out of every generated dashboard.
data_description.excluded_entities: These entities are left out of every generated dashboard, regardless of area.
data_description.require_confirmation: When a scan produces a changed dashboard, hold off writing it and raise a Repairs issue with a diff instead - nothing is applied until you confirm it there.
data_description.storage_mode: Register dashboards directly in Home Assistant's UI storage instead of writing YAML files that need a one-time configuration.yaml paste. Relies on undocumented Home Assistant internals - falls back to file-only mode automatically if it fails.

scan.name: Scan
scan.description: Re-discover areas, devices and entities and rebuild the internal dashboard graph immediately, instead of waiting for the next scheduled or registry-triggered scan. Also regenerates dashboards once the scan completes.

generate.name: Generate
generate.description: Recompile the Home, Rooms, Homelab, Security, Monitoring, Admin and Cloud dashboards from the current discovery graph and write them to the dashboards/ folder, without doing a fresh scan first.

view.name: View
view.description: Optional - only recompile and write this one dashboard, leaving the others untouched. Omit to regenerate all dashboards.

missing_frontend_resources.title: Some dashboard cards need HACS frontend resources
missing_frontend_resources.description: The generated dashboards use {missing} for some cards, which aren't detected as installed Lovelace resources yet. Install them via HACS → Frontend, then wait for the next scan (or call `ha_auto_dashboard.generate`) to switch those cards over. Dashboards still work without them - the affected cards just use a plain built-in fallback in the meantime.

register_dashboards.title: Generated dashboards need one paste into configuration.yaml
register_dashboards.description:
Add this to `configuration.yaml` and restart Home Assistant to register the generated dashboards (one-time step - future scans just rewrite the same files in place):

```yaml
{snippet}
```

pending_dashboard_change.title: Generated dashboards have pending changes
pending_dashboard_change.description: The next scan produced a different dashboard than what's on disk. Nothing has been written yet - review the diff and confirm to apply it.
